creator: sgk@troutmask.apl.washington.edu
text:
On Mon, Mar 09, 2009 at 12:27:34PM +0100, Hans Petter Selasky wrote:
> Hi,
> 
> The patch looks like a compile fix only ...
> 
> Some thoughts.
> 
> 1) LibUSB0.1 should really not need to be installed any more on FreeBSD-8 
> current except for the CPP part which I think is not used?
> 
> 2) LibUSB0.1 port can check if libusb20 is present and install the 
> documentation, if any. Also the LibUSB0.1 port can compile the libusb0.1 
> tests.
> 
> 
> Here are some patches which do not work 100% because I'm not a autoconf 
> expert, but it might give some clues:
> 
> 

|diff -u -r libusb-0.1.12.orig/Makefile.am libusb-0.1.12/Makefile.am
|--- libusb-0.1.12.orig/Makefile.am      Mon Mar  9 09:00:25 2009
|+++ libusb-0.1.12/Makefile.am   Mon Mar  9 11:53:34 2009
--------------------------
Patching file libusb-0.1.12/Makefile.am using Plan A...
Hunk #1 succeeded at 23.
Hunk #2 failed at 44.
Hunk #3 succeeded at 63.
1 out of 3 hunks failed--saving rejects to libusb-0.1.12/Makefile.am.rej
Hmm...  The next patch looks like a unified diff to me...
The text leading up to this was:
--------------------------
|
|
|
|diff -u -r libusb-0.1.12.orig/configure.in libusb-0.1.12/configure.in
|--- libusb-0.1.12.orig/configure.in     Mon Mar  9 09:00:25 2009
|+++ libusb-0.1.12/configure.in  Mon Mar  9 12:13:35 2009
--------------------------
Patching file libusb-0.1.12/configure.in using Plan A...
Hunk #1 succeeded at 11.
Hunk #2 succeeded at 129.
Hunk #3 succeeded at 189.
patch: **** malformed patch at line 150: versions.

-- 
Steve
